images.caption: Figure 1:Comparison on SWE-bench Verified and LongBench-v2 Multi-Doc QA.Our method,DeLM, achieves the best average performance across both agentic and long-context benchmarks.

Figure 2:Centralized vs. decentralized multi-agent systems.Centralized MAS relies on a main agent to assign subcontexts, spawn sub-agents, and integrate returned results through a synchronous scatter–gather loop, creating a bottleneck where progress is gated by both the central merge step and the slowest worker. In contrast,DeLMdecentralizes coordination through parallel agents, a shared context, and a task queue, allowing agents to exchange progress through shared state, asynchronously claim ready tasks, and scale more adaptively as the number of subtasks grows.

Figure 3:Overview ofDeLM.A one-time initialization step decomposes the input into initial subtasks and places them in a shared task queue. Parallel agents asynchronously claim tasks (Ti{T_{i}}), read the verified shared context, and perform local reasoning. Completed updates are compressed, verified, and admitted as compact gists (Gi{G_{i}}), making reusable progress visible to all agents. When the task queue becomes empty, the most recently completed agent checks the existing subtask states and shared context to decide whether additional subtasks are needed. If so, it generates and enqueues new tasks for another round of parallel execution; otherwise, it produces the final answer.

Figure 4:Ablation and robustness analysis on LongBench-v2 Multi-Doc QA.All bars report accuracy averaged over the five domains with GPT-5.4. (a) Modular ablation: removing either the verification step or the hierarchical summary lowers accuracy. (b, c)DeLMis largely insensitive to its gist configuration: accuracy is stable once the gist is long enough (b) and across summarizers of varying cost (c). In every panel, the darker bar marks the default configuration used in Table2.

Figure 5:(a) Long source units are compressed into reference-grounded summariesSiS_{i}and then compact gistsGiG_{i}stored in the shared context. (b) Agents read gists by default, selectively unfold them to summaries or raw evidence when needed, and (c) admit entries only after verification.
